description: Samsung's most powerful S-series phone with built-in S Pen, titanium frame, and AI-powered camera system. The Galaxy S24 Ultra features a 200MP main camera, Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 processor, and the brightest Galaxy display ever with 2600 nits peak brightness. Galaxy AI features include Circle to Search, Live Translate, and Generative Edit.
mainCamera: 200MP f/1.7 + 50MP f/3.4 Periscope 5x + 10MP f/2.4 3x Telephoto + 12MP f/2.2 Ultrawide
cameraFeatures: 8K video, 4K 120fps, Super Slow-mo, Nightography, ProVisual Engine, AI Zoom
build: Titanium frame, Gorilla Glass Armor front/back
colors: Titanium Gray, Titanium Black, Titanium Violet, Titanium Yellow, Titanium Blue, Titanium Green
specialFeatures: Built-in S Pen, Galaxy AI, Circle to Search, Live Translate, Note Assist, Chat Assist, Titanium Frame
pros: Best display in any phone, S Pen built-in, 7 years updates, AI features, Titanium build, 5x optical zoom
cons: Heavy at 232g, 45W charging is slow vs competition, no charger in box, expensive
reviewSummary: The Galaxy S24 Ultra is the most complete flagship phone of 2024. Its 200MP camera system delivers class-leading photos in all conditions, the Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 provides excellent gaming performance, and Galaxy AI brings genuinely useful features. The built-in S Pen and titanium frame make it a productivity powerhouse. However, the 45W charging speed lags behind Chinese competitors offering 100W+.
reviewVerdict: Best overall flagship for power users who want camera, productivity, and AI in one package.

description: Apple's ultimate iPhone with A17 Pro chip, titanium design, and the best video recording in any smartphone. Features a 5x optical zoom telephoto camera, USB-C with USB 3 speeds, and Action Button for customizable shortcuts.
gpu: Apple GPU (6-core) with hardware ray tracing
mainCamera: 48MP f/1.78 Main + 12MP f/2.2 Ultrawide + 12MP f/2.8 5x Telephoto
cameraFeatures: ProRAW, ProRes video, Log encoding, Spatial Video, Smart HDR 5, Photonic Engine
build: Titanium frame, Ceramic Shield front, Matte glass back
sensors: LiDAR Scanner, Accelerometer, Gyro, Proximity, Compass, Barometer
colors: Natural Titanium, Blue Titanium, White Titanium, Black Titanium
specialFeatures: Action Button, USB 3.0, A17 Pro with console-level gaming, Spatial Video recording
pros: Best video recording, A17 Pro performance, Titanium build, excellent ecosystem, 5x zoom, USB-C
cons: Very expensive, only 27W charging, no always-on display option, 120Hz only on Pro models, heavy
reviewSummary: The iPhone 15 Pro Max delivers the best smartphone video experience and top-tier performance with the A17 Pro chip. The new 5x tetraprism telephoto camera is a significant upgrade, and the titanium design feels premium while being lighter. However, the 27W charging speed is disappointingly slow compared to Android flagships, and the high price in Pakistan makes it a tough value proposition.
reviewVerdict: Best choice for iOS users and content creators who prioritize video quality and ecosystem integration.

description: Samsung's best mid-range phone with premium glass back, 4 years of OS updates, and IP67 water resistance. The Exynos 1480 chip handles daily tasks and moderate gaming with ease, while the 50MP OIS camera takes great photos for the price.
mainCamera: 50MP f/1.8 OIS + 12MP f/2.2 Ultrawide + 5MP f/2.4 Macro
build: Glass back (Gorilla Glass Victus+), Aluminum frame
colors: Awesome Iceblue, Awesome Lilac, Awesome Lemon, Awesome Navy
specialFeatures: Glass back design, IP67, 4 years updates, Samsung Knox, Samsung Pay
pros: Premium glass build, IP67 water resistance, great display, 4 years OS updates, good main camera
cons: Only 25W charging, Exynos not great for gaming, no charger in box, thick bezels, heavy
reviewSummary: The Galaxy A55 is Samsung's most premium mid-ranger, offering a glass back, IP67 rating, and 4 years of updates that are rare in this segment. The Super AMOLED display is vibrant, and the 50MP camera with OIS takes excellent daylight photos. However, the 25W charging is slow, and the Exynos 1480 can't handle heavy gaming well.
reviewVerdict: Best mid-range Samsung phone for users who want premium build quality and long software support.

description: Xiaomi's photography flagship co-engineered with Leica, featuring a massive 1-inch sensor main camera with variable aperture, and a professional-grade camera system with four 50MP lenses. Runs on Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 with HyperOS.
mainCamera: 50MP f/1.63-f/4.0 Variable Aperture (1-inch) + 50MP f/1.8 Periscope 5x + 50MP f/1.8 3.2x Telephoto + 12MP f/2.2 Ultrawide
cameraFeatures: Leica Summilux lenses, Leica Authentic/Vibrant modes, 8K video, 4K 120fps, Dolby Vision, Variable Aperture
build: Aluminum frame, Eco-leather back, Ceramic Shield front
sensors: Accelerometer, Gyro, Proximity, Compass, Barometer, Color sensor
specialFeatures: Leica camera system, Variable Aperture, 90W+80W charging, Eco-leather back
pros: Best camera system with 1-inch sensor and variable aperture, Leica tuning, 90W+80W charging, great display
cons: Eco-leather not for everyone, large camera bump, PTA not approved, expensive
reviewSummary: The Xiaomi 14 Ultra is a photographer's dream phone. The 1-inch Sony LYT-900 sensor with Leica optics produces stunning images with beautiful bokeh and natural colors. The variable aperture (f/1.63-f/4.0) is a unique feature that gives real creative control. With 90W wired and 80W wireless charging, battery anxiety is non-existent.
reviewVerdict: Best camera phone for photography enthusiasts. Leica co-engineering makes this a genuine alternative to professional cameras.

description: Infinix's premium mid-range offering with wireless charging (rare in this segment), 108MP main camera, AMOLED display with 120Hz, and 68W fast charging. A feature-packed phone that punches above its price.
colors: Titan Gold, Obsidian Black, Vintage Green
specialFeatures: Wireless Charging (rare at this price), MagSafe-like accessories, JBL-tuned speakers
pros: Wireless charging at this price, 108MP camera, 68W charging, AMOLED 120Hz, MagSafe ecosystem, 2TB SD card
cons: No 5G, Helio G99 is weak for gaming, no OIS on camera, plastic frame, USB 2.0 only
reviewSummary: The Infinix Note 40 Pro is a value champion with wireless charging and MagSafe support, features unheard of at PKR 57K. The 108MP camera takes good daylight shots and the 68W charging is fast. However, the Helio G99 processor limits gaming performance and the lack of 5G is a drawback for future-proofing.
reviewVerdict: Best value mid-ranger for users who want premium features like wireless charging without the flagship price tag.

description: Samsung's affordable mid-ranger with Super AMOLED display, IP67 water resistance, and 4 years of software updates. Great for users who want Samsung reliability and a clean Android experience on a budget.
mainCamera: 50MP f/1.8 OIS + 8MP f/2.2 Ultrawide + 5MP f/2.4 Macro
build: Glass back (Gorilla Glass Victus+), Plastic frame
colors: Awesome Iceblue, Awesome Lilac, Awesome Lemon, Awesome Navy
pros: IP67, 4 years updates, Super AMOLED 120Hz, compact design, OIS camera
cons: 25W charging, Exynos 1380 is basic, no charger in box, thick bezels, no 5G on some variants
reviewSummary: The Galaxy A35 offers Samsung's trademark reliability with IP67 water resistance and 4 years of updates at a competitive price. The Super AMOLED display is bright and smooth, and the 50MP camera with OIS takes good photos. It's not a gaming powerhouse, but for everyday use it's a solid choice.
reviewVerdict: Best budget Samsung phone with premium features like IP67 and long-term software support.

description: Tecno's battery and gaming focused phone with massive 6000mAh battery, 70W fast charging, and a unique LED light strip on the back. The Dimensity 6080 handles gaming well, making it a great budget gaming phone.
colors: Magnet Black, Comet Green, Meteorite Grey
specialFeatures: Dynamic Port LED strip, 6000mAh battery, 70W charging
pros: 6000mAh battery, 70W fast charging, LED light strip, 108MP camera, affordable price
cons: IPS LCD display, no 5G, plastic build, average cameras, no OIS
reviewSummary: The Tecno Pova 6 Pro is a battery beast with 6000mAh and 70W charging that lasts 2 days easily. The unique LED light strip adds personality, and the 108MP camera is decent for the price. Best suited for users who prioritize battery life and fast charging over display quality.
reviewVerdict: Best battery phone under 50K PKR. Great for heavy users and gamers on a budget.

description: OnePlus flagship with Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, Hasselblad camera partnership, and 100W SUPERVOOC charging. Features one of the brightest displays at 4500 nits and a 5400mAh battery that charges from 0-100% in just 26 minutes.
mainCamera: 50MP f/1.7 Sony LYT-808 OIS + 48MP f/2.6 3x Periscope Telephoto + 64MP f/2.2 Ultrawide
cameraFeatures: Hasselblad tuning, Hasselblad Natural/Vivid, 4K Dolby Vision, RAW+
sensors: Accelerometer, Gyro, Proximity, Compass, Color Temperature sensor
specialFeatures: 100W+50W charging, Hasselblad camera, 4500 nits display, Alert Slider
pros: 100W charging in 26 min, 4500 nits display, Hasselblad cameras, Alert Slider, OxygenOS clean UI
cons: No IP68 (only IP65), camera bump, no expandable storage, PTA not approved
reviewSummary: The OnePlus 12 is the fastest-charging flagship with 100W wired and 50W wireless charging, going from 0-100% in just 26 minutes. The 4500-nit 2K display is stunningly bright, and the Hasselblad-tuned camera system delivers punchy, satisfying photos. OxygenOS 14 remains one of the cleanest Android skins available.
reviewVerdict: Best charging flagship with clean software. Ideal for users who value speed, display quality, and fast charging.

description: Realme's performance flagship killer with Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3, 120W fast charging, and a 5500mAh battery. Offers flagship-level performance at mid-range pricing with AI features and a premium LTPO AMOLED display.
specialFeatures: AI Night Vision, AI Smart Loop, 120W charging, Rainwater Smart Touch
pros: Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3 performance, 120W charging, 5500mAh battery, LTPO display, AI features
cons: Only 2 cameras, plastic frame, USB 2.0, no IP rating, no charger in box
reviewSummary: The Realme GT 6 is a performance monster at PKR 110K. The Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3 delivers near-flagship gaming performance, and the 120W charging fills the massive 5500mAh battery in minutes. The LTPO AMOLED display is excellent. However, the camera system is basic with only two lenses, and the lack of IP rating and USB 3.0 are noticeable omissions.
reviewVerdict: Best performance-to-price ratio in Pakistan. Ideal for gamers and power users on a budget.

description: Samsung's compact flagship with Galaxy AI features, Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 (in select regions), and a premium flat design. The 6.2-inch display makes it one of the best small flagship phones available.
mainCamera: 50MP f/1.8 OIS + 12MP f/2.2 Ultrawide + 10MP f/2.4 3x Telephoto
cameraFeatures: 8K video, 4K 60fps, Nightography, ProVisual Engine, Galaxy AI
build: Glass back, Aluminum frame (Gorilla Glass Victus 2)
colors: Onyx Black, Marble Gray, Cobalt Violet, Amber Yellow, Jade Green, Sandstone Orange
specialFeatures: Galaxy AI, Circle to Search, Live Translate, compact design, 7 years updates
pros: Compact and lightweight (167g), Galaxy AI, 7 years updates, IP68, great cameras
cons: 4000mAh battery is small, only 25W charging, no charger in box, FHD+ display (not 2K)
reviewSummary: The Galaxy S24 is the best compact flagship of 2024. At just 167g with a 6.2-inch display, it's perfect for one-handed use. Galaxy AI features like Circle to Search and Live Translate are genuinely useful. The 3x optical zoom and Nightography cameras are excellent. The main trade-off is the 4000mAh battery which needs a top-up by evening for heavy users.
reviewVerdict: Best small flagship phone. Perfect for users who want premium features in a compact, lightweight design.

description: Xiaomi's popular mid-range phone with 200MP main camera, Snapdragon 7s Gen 2, and a beautiful 120Hz AMOLED display. The 200MP sensor captures incredible detail and the 5100mAh battery with 67W charging keeps you going all day.
colors: Midnight Black, Aurora Purple, Ocean Teal
specialFeatures: 200MP camera, IP54, IR blaster, 3.5mm jack, 67W charging
pros: 200MP camera with OIS, 120Hz AMOLED, 67W charging, 3.5mm jack, IR blaster, SD card slot
cons: Snapdragon 7s Gen 2 is mid-tier, MIUI bloatware, USB 2.0, plastic frame
reviewSummary: The Redmi Note 13 Pro 5G offers the highest megapixel count in its class with the 200MP ISOCELL HP3 sensor. In good lighting, the detail is impressive. The 120Hz AMOLED display is vibrant, and 67W charging is fast. However, MIUI has bloatware and the Snapdragon 7s Gen 2 is adequate but not exceptional for gaming.
reviewVerdict: Best camera mid-ranger for detail lovers. 200MP sensor and OIS at this price is unbeatable.

description: Apple's standard flagship with Dynamic Island, USB-C, A16 Bionic chip, and 48MP main camera. The iPhone 15 brings Pro-level features like the 48MP camera and Dynamic Island to the base model.
build: Glass back, Aluminum frame, Ceramic Shield
pros: iOS ecosystem, A16 Bionic performance, 48MP camera, Dynamic Island, USB-C, compact design
cons: 60Hz display, only 20W charging, base storage 128GB, expensive for the specs
reviewSummary: The iPhone 15 brings welcome upgrades to the base model including the 48MP camera, Dynamic Island, and USB-C. The A16 Bionic is still powerful and iOS 17 runs smoothly. However, the 60Hz display is a significant omission at this price, and the 20W charging is slow. Best for users already in the Apple ecosystem.
reviewVerdict: Best entry point to iOS. Recommended for ecosystem users who don't need Pro features.

description: Infinix's dedicated gaming phone with MediaTek Dimensity 8200 Ultimate, 120Hz AMOLED display, and cyberpunk-inspired design with LED lighting. Features a vapor chamber cooling system and dedicated gaming triggers for competitive mobile gaming.
build: Glass back with LED strip, Plastic frame
colors: Mecha Blue, Mecha Orange, Mecha Silver
specialFeatures: Gaming triggers, Vapor chamber cooling, RGB LED lighting, XBOOST gaming engine
pros: Dimensity 8200 gaming performance, 5G, AMOLED 120Hz, gaming triggers, RGB lighting, affordable
cons: Average cameras, no OIS, USB 2.0, only 45W charging, no IP rating
reviewSummary: The Infinix GT 20 Pro is Pakistan's most affordable dedicated gaming phone. The Dimensity 8200 handles PUBG and COD Mobile at 60fps easily, and the physical gaming triggers give a competitive edge. The cyberpunk design with LED lighting is eye-catching. Cameras are average but gamers don't care about that.
reviewVerdict: Best budget gaming phone in Pakistan. Perfect for competitive mobile gamers on a tight budget.

description: Tecno's budget phone with 108MP main camera, 120Hz display, and 33W fast charging. Offers premium-looking design with vegan leather back at an affordable price point.
colors: Glossy White, Caramel Gold, Dark Web
pros: 108MP camera, vegan leather back, 256GB storage, 33W charging, 120Hz display, affordable
cons: IPS LCD, Helio G99, no 5G, no OIS, USB 2.0
reviewSummary: The Tecno Spark 20 Pro+ looks premium with its vegan leather back and offers a 108MP camera and 256GB storage at just PKR 33K. It's a solid budget choice for users who prioritize storage and camera resolution. The Helio G99 handles daily tasks well but gaming performance is limited.
reviewVerdict: Best budget phone under 35K for users who want premium design, big storage, and decent camera.

description: Samsung's budget king with massive 6000mAh battery, Super AMOLED display, and 5G connectivity. One of the cheapest ways to get a Samsung AMOLED+5G combination in Pakistan.
pros: 6000mAh battery (2 days), Super AMOLED, 5G, One UI, Samsung reliability, 3 years updates
cons: Only 25W charging, Dimensity 6100+ is basic, plastic build, no charger in box, thick bezels
reviewSummary: The Galaxy M15 5G is the best budget Samsung phone for battery life. The 6000mAh battery easily lasts 2 days, and the Super AMOLED display is a rare find at this price. The 5G support future-proofs it. Performance is basic but sufficient for daily tasks, social media, and casual use.
reviewVerdict: Best battery life under 40K. Ideal for users who want Samsung AMOLED + 5G on a tight budget.
